# host_mac_dock_app (Resource)

Manages one application in the macOS Dock persistent apps section.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_brew" "google_chrome" {
  name         = "google-chrome"
  package_type = "cask"
}

resource "host_mac_dock_app" "system_settings" {
  path     = "/System/Applications/System Settings.app"
  priority = 10
}

resource "host_mac_dock_app" "google_chrome" {
  path     = host_package_brew.google_chrome.app_path
  priority = 20
}
```

## Import

Import an existing Dock app by `priority,path`. The path must already be present in the live Dock:

```shell
terraform import host_mac_dock_app.system_settings '10,/System/Applications/System Settings.app'
```

If the priority is omitted, the provider derives it from the app's live Dock order in increments of 10.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `path` (String) Application `.app` bundle path to show in the Dock. `~` is expanded to the provider `home_dir` and relative paths are resolved from the Terraform working directory.
- `priority` (Number) Ordering priority within this Dock section. Lower numbers appear first. Priorities must be unique within apps or folders.

### Optional

- `restart` (Boolean) Restart Dock with `killall Dock` after writes or deletes.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Stable identifier for this managed Dock item.
- `path_resolved` (String) Resolved absolute Dock item path.
